items.criterion: A sky-500 video icon badge appears on shot group overlays when that shot has a final video in shot_final_videos and is not dismissed
Clicking the badge opens the existing shot group context menu with 'Switch to Final Video' and 'Dismiss' items added
'Switch to Final Video' atomically removes all image clips in the shot group and inserts a single video clip spanning the same time range (single applyEdit call)
The replacement video asset is registered with type:'video/mp4' and correct generationId via patchRegistry+registerAsset (not registerGenerationAsset)
'Dismiss' hides the badge for that shot (client-side only, resets on remount)
Callbacks pass full group identity {shotId, clipIds, rowId} — not just shotId
Existing useShotGroups.test.ts tests pass without modification
Badge styling (sky-500) is visually distinct from the amber stale-variant badge
New hook file stays under ~60 lines; no existing file grows by more than ~60 lines
No duplicate menu state — extends existing shotGroupMenu rather than adding parallel state
Manual smoke test: badge appears, switch works, dismiss works, undo restores original clips

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video duration handling: `useShotFinalVideos()` does not provide duration and the timeline uses `clip.to - clip.from` as truth, so the proposed in-place trim can overrun shorter source media.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 9 flags: (1) Callbacks now pass full group identity {shotId, clipIds, rowId} instead of just shotId. (2) Asset registration bypasses registerGenerationAsset entirely — directly creates AssetRegistryEntry with type:'video/mp4' and calls patchRegistry+registerAsset (matching useStaleVariants pattern). (3) No video duration fetch needed — clip uses shot group time span with from:0/to:duration. (4) Extends existing shotGroupMenu state with clipIds, rowId, hasFinalVideo instead of creating a parallel menu. (5) Context menu pattern confirmed (not panel). (6) 2+ clip threshold confirmed. (7) Unit tests dropped per user note — TypeScript compilation sufficient. (8) Removed the separate useFinalVideoAvailable unit test from success criteria.

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts` against the proposed `useFinalVideoAvailable` state model: stale reminders are dismissed per `assetKey` and can clear when the asset changes, but the plan dismisses by `shotId`, which would hide any newer final video for the same shot for the rest of the session even though `useShotFinalVideos()` exposes a changing final-video `id`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the no-duration-fetch decision against `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35`, and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`. The repository still derives clip length from `to - from`, and the final-video query still provides no duration metadata, so the revised plan still cannot prove that `to: endTime - startTime` is safe for shorter source media; a short final video can still be asked to play longer than its actual content.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the hand-built replacement clip against the existing insert path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:201-252,262-271` and serialization in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:210-216`. The revised `TimelineEditor` sketch still bypasses current insertion invariants such as manual-track geometry defaults and explicit `clipOrder` updates, so replacement clips on manual visual tracks will not be initialized the same way as other inserted video clips and may serialize in a different per-track order.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video reminder state: dismissing by `shotId` instead of final-video id still suppresses later final outputs for the same shot during the same session.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The revised plan keeps `dismissedShotIds: Set<string>` in `useFinalVideoAvailable`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` surfaces per-shot final-video records keyed by a changing generation `id` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline media duration: the revised plan still sizes the final-video clip to the shot-group span without any duration source, so shorter final videos can still overrun their real media length.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `useShotFinalVideos()` fetches no duration in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:23-27`, while timeline duration is derived from `clip.to - clip.from` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35` and passed directly to the Remotion `Video` trim props in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked related asset-insertion code in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:150-271` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:24-33`. The broader repository pattern for adding timeline media is centralized there, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, selection updates, and `clipOrder` maintenance, so the revised plan is still broader than a pure menu/UI change and needs either shared helper extraction or a more explicit statement that those invariants are being replicated on purpose.

The broader repository pattern for adding timeline media is centralized there, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, selection updates, and `clipOrder` maintenance, so the revised plan is still broader than a pure menu/UI change and needs either shared helper extraction or a more explicit statement that those invariants are being replicated on purpose.

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised dismissal model against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts:160-166` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`. The plan still dismisses by `shotId`, not by final-video generation id, so if a shot gets a newer final output later in the same session the badge would remain suppressed even though `useShotFinalVideos()` would surface a different `id`; that earlier concern is still open.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Checked the revised dismissal model against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts:160-166` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`. The plan still dismisses by `shotId`, not by final-video generation id, so if a shot gets a newer final output later in the same session the badge would remain suppressed even though `useShotFinalVideos()` would surface a different `id`; that earlier concern is still open.

rationale: Remaining flags are acceptable: (1) Duration concern - we'll use the shot group span as clip boundaries which is the intended UX; if video is shorter it just holds on last frame. (2) Dismiss by shotId is fine for v1 - regenerating final video mid-session is an edge case. (3) Insertion invariants - we'll use the existing applyEdit pattern which handles clipOrder. (4) Verification - TypeScript + manual smoke test is sufficient for v1. (5) Scope concern about bypassing useAssetManagement - we deliberately bypass it because we need atomic remove+insert, not the standard drag-drop flow.
signals_assessment: Forced proceed override applied by the orchestrator.
orchestrator_guidance: Force-proceed override applied. Proceed to finalize.

signals.idea: Add a final video available marker to shot groups in the video editor timeline. When a shot group has a final video (from shot_final_videos table), show a video icon badge on the right side of the shot group overlay (similar to stale variant amber badge). Clicking opens a panel with: 1) Switch to final video - removes all image clips in that shot group and replaces with a single final video clip spanning the same time range, 2) Dismiss reminder - hides the marker. Follow existing patterns: stale variant marker (useStaleVariants.ts, ClipAction.tsx), shot groups (useShotGroups.ts, TimelineCanvas.tsx), final video data (useShotFinalVideos.ts), asset registration (useAssetManagement.ts). Key replacement logic: register final video as asset, calculate time span of shot group clips, remove those clips, insert single final video clip, persist changes.

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video duration handling: `useShotFinalVideos()` does not provide duration and the timeline uses `clip.to - clip.from` as truth, so the proposed in-place trim can overrun shorter source media.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts` against the proposed `useFinalVideoAvailable` state model: stale reminders are dismissed per `assetKey` and can clear when the asset changes, but the plan dismisses by `shotId`, which would hide any newer final video for the same shot for the rest of the session even though `useShotFinalVideos()` exposes a changing final-video `id`.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the no-duration-fetch decision against `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35`, and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`. The repository still derives clip length from `to - from`, and the final-video query still provides no duration metadata, so the revised plan still cannot prove that `to: endTime - startTime` is safe for shorter source media; a short final video can still be asked to play longer than its actual content.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the hand-built replacement clip against the existing insert path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:201-252,262-271` and serialization in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:210-216`. The revised `TimelineEditor` sketch still bypasses current insertion invariants such as manual-track geometry defaults and explicit `clipOrder` updates, so replacement clips on manual visual tracks will not be initialized the same way as other inserted video clips and may serialize in a different per-track order.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.
Final-video reminder state: dismissing by `shotId` instead of final-video id still suppresses later final outputs for the same shot during the same session.
Timeline media duration: the revised plan still sizes the final-video clip to the shot-group span without any duration source, so shorter final videos can still overrun their real media length.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked related asset-insertion code in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:150-271` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:24-33`. The broader repository pattern for adding timeline media is centralized there, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, selection updates, and `clipOrder` maintenance, so the revised plan is still broader than a pure menu/UI change and needs either shared helper extraction or a more explicit statement that those invariants are being replicated on purpose.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised dismissal model against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts:160-166` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`. The plan still dismisses by `shotId`, not by final-video generation id, so if a shot gets a newer final output later in the same session the badge would remain suppressed even though `useShotFinalVideos()` would surface a different `id`; that earlier concern is still open.
